title: AutoResearch: The Future of Autonomous Recursive Self-Improvement
covers: AutoResearch architecture (Karpathy's 3-file design: program.md/train.py/prepare.py); recursive self-improvement mechanism (closed-loop: hypothesis→implementation→evaluation→commit/reset); multi-agent research pipelines (AutoResearchClaw 23-stage, AI Scientist-v2 agentic tree search, Sparks proposer-critic, RoboPhD ELO selection); improvement mechanisms (proposer-critic loops, dual-memory systems, MetaClaw cross-run learning, self-healing frameworks); RSI limitations (self-improvement paradox, inability to self-correct reasoning, GV-Gap saturation within 2-3 rounds, data autophagy/model collapse, small model barrier <7B, theoretical limits: Munchausen/Löb/Procrastination); literature review at scale (PICO planning, OpenAlex 474M+ papers, Elicit 99.4% accuracy, Consensus Meter, 4-layer citation verification, BLEU/ROUGE/Jaccard metrics); governance and safety (Athens Roundtable 2025 red lines: no autonomous weight updates, no infrastructure access, no steganographic collusion; IVOs, cryptographic commitments; alignment degradation, responsibility gap, sandbagging risks)

title: NotebookLM add-research Deduplication Behavior
covers: `add-research` does NOT deduplicate across sessions (H1 confirmed); no dedup flags on `add-research` — `--import-all` auto-imports but does not filter (H2 partially confirmed); server does NOT enforce dedup natively — client handles idempotency only on error recovery, not proactively (H3 confirmed); idempotency hardening = probe-then-retry on network failure only (not cross-session dedup); `source clean` command = primary dedup tool (flags: --dry-run, -y/--yes, --json, -n, --all/--latest/--earliest); pasted text sources are explicitly non-idempotent (no server-side dedup key); recommended workflow: add-research → source clean --dry-run → source clean --yes; 50-source limit makes clean essential

title: NotebookLM source add URL Acceptance Conditions
covers: H1 NOT CONFIRMED — no evidence tracking params (utm_*, fbclid, ref=) cause rejection; ?si= YouTube case (C-017) is YouTube-specific, not general param rule; H2 CANNOT CONFIRM — URL shortener/redirect behavior undocumented; H3 CONFIRMED — no domain allowlist, rejection is content-based (attempt-to-fetch model); X.com accepted but silently misparsed (returns error page as content); paywalled/login-required = failed-to-load after attempt; HTML/XHTML file upload = hard rejection; JS-dependent sites = crawl failure; files >20MB = upload timeout; .txt/.md files intermittent "return None" bug; workarounds: pre-fetch to Markdown, manual extraction, pandoc/lynx, add_text API; Mint pipeline implications: strip ?si= from YouTube (C-017), avoid X.com, pre-screen paywall domains, use source clean for error-state detection

title: notebooklm-py vs Official NotebookLM API
covers: H1 CONFIRMED — Google has no official public API for consumer NotebookLM; notebooklm-py reverse-engineers batchexecute RPC protocol (same protocol used by web frontend); 6-character obfuscated method IDs, triple-nested JSON arrays, browser cookie + CSRF token auth; Enterprise API exists since 2026 but requires Google Cloud + enterprise license; H2 CONFIRMED — undocumented endpoints have no stability guarantees; silent RPC ID rotation is primary failure mode; schema drift (positional JSON arrays) secondary; nightly GitHub Action (rpc-health.yml) monitors 35+ RPC methods, auto-creates `rpc-breakage` issues; `NOTEBOOKLM_RPC_OVERRIDES` env var for self-patching; exponential backoff built-in; H3 CONFIRMED — documentation lag is asymmetrical: notebooklm-py documents hidden features (batch downloads, mind map JSON, source fulltext) that Google never published; rpc-reference.md archives legacy/experimental IDs; Enterprise API = REST surface (licensed orgs only); pipeline implications: wrap ask in try/catch for UnknownRPCMethodError, use NOTEBOOKLM_RPC_OVERRIDES for immediate recovery, C-005 covers cookie expiry, one client per event loop (not thread-safe)
